evidence.scientific_clarity: Pipeline flows clearly: Query→Retriever↔KB (Retrieval group) then DiffusionDecoder+PriorNetwork feedback (Generation group)→Output; solid vs dashed arrows correctly encode data-flow vs control.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type3 (bitmapped, non-selectable) glyphs; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default, not a preferred journal font.
evidence.palette: Green input / red output used directionally at node level (intentional); neutral white for modules, light-purple for datastore; blue/orange group backgrounds are colorblind-distinguishable, but overall scheme is workable, not optimal.
evidence.layout_density: Panel space used reasonably well; nodes are well-spaced and non-overlapping; small dead zone to the right of 'Generated Sample'; legend fits comfortably in lower-left.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes both group shadings, all nodes, and both arrow types; no numerical claim or crisp one-sentence finding, but acceptable for a pure architecture diagram.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists but fonts are embedded as Type3 (bitmapped); text is not selectable/editable; fonttype=42 was omitted from the save call.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py; figure.svg also present as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all components and explains shading and arrow conventions clearly; lacks a concise headline finding or stage count, but is otherwise informative.

overall_strengths: Scientific content is complete and correctly encoded: all pipeline stages, group boundaries, and control/feedback semantics are faithfully represented.
All required output files are present; the generation script is clean and data-driven (reads data.json), making the figure reproducible.
overall_weaknesses: Vector fidelity is broken: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) were not set, so the PDF and SVG embed bitmapped Type3 glyphs instead of editable TrueType/OTF outlines.
Typography uses matplotlib's default DejaVu Sans throughout with no explicit rcParams for fonttype, making the figure unsuitable for direct journal submission without regeneration.
